name: Potential Homoglyph Usage
notes:
This rule triggers when a domain name contains a homoglyph character(s), which could make a domain appear the same as a trusted domain and redirects to a malicious host.
The custom function HOMOGLYPH::DETECTED, which is pre-filled with 1792 entries of homoglyph characters, accepts a string and returns true if the string contains a homoglyph character.

Note: To view the events that would trigger this rule, use the search "Potential Homoglyph Usage". Tune the rule with valid characters before enabling the rule and offense creation.
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Proxy, BB:DeviceDefinition: DNS
when the event context is Local to Local
when the event matches URL Host (custom) is not N/A
when the event matches LOWER("URL Host") like 'Ω' AQL filter query
when the event matches HOMOGLYPH::DETECTED("URL Host") == TRUE AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Potential Homoglyph Usage on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Proxy, BB:DeviceDefinition: DNS
AND NOT when the event context is Local to Local
AND when the event matches URL Host (custom) is not N/A
AND NOT when the event matches LOWER("URL Host") like 'Ω' AQL filter query
AND when the event matches HOMOGLYPH::DETECTED("URL Host") == TRUE AQL filter query
RED_list: Event description: An event with a domain name containing a homoglyph character has been detected.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using URL Host (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
